user_query:
I host a small weekly storytelling circle, and I often share memories from the bullfights I witnessed in my youth. Please set up a small, automatic weekly task that searches the Internet for two reputable background sources on the history and terminology of bullfighting (tauromachia), and then compiles meeting notes with agenda and action items for our group.

Inputs (already provided in the workspace):
- input/search_queries.yaml
- input/club_members.csv
- input/agenda_topics.txt

What I need you to build and deliver:
1) Implement a runnable pipeline script that reads input/search_queries.yaml and:
   - Performs the specified search engine queries on the Internet.
   - Chooses at least two distinct sources from reputable domains only: either top-level domains .edu or .org, or domains that clearly belong to cultural institutions (domain name contains "museum" or "culture"/"cultura"). Do not hardcode URLs; use a general-purpose search engine and record what you found.
   - For each chosen source, capture: title, url, domain, accessed_at (ISO 8601), query_used, and a short snippet (up to ~280 characters from the page or search result snippet). Remove duplicate URLs.
   - Save your results to both files:
     - output/search_results.json (array of objects with the fields above)
     - output/search_results.csv (columns: title,url,domain,accessed_at,query_used,snippet)

2) Prepare meeting notes using both the inputs and the search results:
   - Create output/meeting_notes.md with sections:
     - Date: today’s date.
     - Agenda: list every line from input/agenda_topics.txt.
     - Background Sources: table or list summarizing the sources from output/search_results.json (at least two) with title, domain, and accessed_at; include the URL for each.
     - Action Items: at least 3 items, each with an owner assigned in round-robin order from input/club_members.csv, a due date of the next Monday, and status "Planned". Tie each item to the background sources or agenda (e.g., “Verify definition of ‘corrida’ from Source X”, “Find a historic photo of a plaza mentioned in Agenda”).

3) Scheduling (weekly automation at 09:00 local time every Monday):
   - Provide OS-appropriate scheduler artifacts without assuming I’ll run install commands immediately. Create:
     - For macOS/Linux: output/cron_entry.txt containing a single crontab line that runs your pipeline weekly at 09:00 on Mondays and logs to output/last_run.log.
     - For Windows: output/weekly_task.xml for Task Scheduler that runs the same pipeline weekly at 09:00 on Mondays and logs to output/last_run.log.
   - Also provide a small wrapper to run the pipeline manually and via the scheduler:
     - scripts/run_pipeline.(py|sh|bat) — your choice, but make sure it executes the full workflow above and writes logs to output/last_run.log. If you use Python, keep any third-party dependencies minimal and document them.
   - Include output/README.md with brief instructions for installing the cron entry (macOS/Linux) or importing the Task Scheduler XML (Windows), and a one-line example to run it manually.

4) Test run and analyze output:
   - Perform one test run now to generate the artifacts. Capture all stdout/stderr to output/last_run.log.
   - Create output/run_summary.json with: started_at, finished_at, sources_found (integer), and errors (array of error message lines extracted from the log; empty if none).
   - If there were any errors or warnings, create output/run_diagnostics.md explaining what failed, how you diagnosed it using the command output or error messages, and what you changed to fix it. If no errors occurred, include a short note stating "No errors" and summarize key observations (e.g., how many sources were found and which domain types they came from).

Notes and constraints:
- The Internet search step is required; do not fabricate results. Choose sources that satisfy the domain rule above, and record the actual domains and titles you found.
- Do not require any accounts or credentials.
- Keep outputs reproducible and clearly structured. Use only the provided input files plus live search results.
- All generated files must be saved under the specified output/ and scripts/ paths so I can verify them locally.

Deliverables summary:
- scripts/run_pipeline.(py|sh|bat)
- output/search_results.json and output/search_results.csv
- output/meeting_notes.md
- output/cron_entry.txt and/or output/weekly_task.xml (provide both if you can)
- output/last_run.log
- output/run_summary.json
- output/run_diagnostics.md (if any issues; otherwise include a brief "No errors" note)
- output/README.md
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
